source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A pendulum of mass $m$ and length $l$ is released from rest in a horizontal position. A nail a distance $d$ below the pivot causes the mass to move along the path indicated by the dotted line. Find the minimum distance $d$ in terms of $l$ such that the mass will swing completely round in the circle shown in Fig. 1.8.
extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL image descriptor string rather than a file path or URL

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A pendulum of mass $m$ and length $l$ is released from rest in a horizontal position. A nail a distance $d$ below the pivot causes the mass to move along the path indicated by the dotted line. Find the minimum distance $d$ in terms of $l$ such that the mass will swing completely round in the circle shown in Fig. 1.8.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL image descriptor string rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: Take the mass $m$ as a point mass. At the instant when the pendulum collides with the nail, $m$ has a velocity $v=\sqrt{2gl}$. The angular momentum of the mass with respect to the point at which the nail locates is conserved during the collision. Then the velocity of the mass is still $v$ at the instant after the collision and the motion thereafter is such that the mass is constrained to rotate around the nail. Under the critical condition that the mass can just swing completely round in a circle, the gravitational force is equal to the centripetal force when the mass is at the top of the circle. Let the velocity of the mass at this instant be $v_{1}$, and we have $\frac{mv_{1}^{2}}{l-d}=mg$ or $v_{1}^{2}=(l-d)g$. The energy equation $\frac{mv^{2}}{2}=\frac{mv_{1}^{2}}{2}+2mg(l-d)$ or $2gl=(l-d)g+4(l-d)g$ then gives the minimum distance as $d=\frac{3l}{5}$.

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: Take the mass $m$ as a point mass. At the instant when the pendulum collides with the nail, $m$ has a velocity $v=\sqrt{2gl}$. The angular momentum of the mass with respect to the point at which the nail locates is conserved during the collision. Then the velocity of the mass is still $v$ at the instant after the collision and the motion thereafter is such that the mass is constrained to rotate around the nail. Under the critical condition that the mass can just swing completely round in a circle, the gravitational force is equal to the centripetal force when the mass is at the top of the circle. Let the velocity of the mass at this instant be $v_{1}$, and we have $\frac{mv_{1}^{2}}{l-d}=mg$ or $v_{1}^{2}=(l-d)g$. The energy equation $\frac{mv^{2}}{2}=\frac{mv_{1}^{2}}{2}+2mg(l-d)$ or $2gl=(l-d)g+4(l-d)g$ then gives the minimum distance as $d=\frac{3l}{5}$.
